title: Pleiotaenia
intro: Pleiotaenia triloba, commonly known as pleiotad, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, native to China and Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cold, and flu. The bioactive constituents of Pleiotad include phenolic glycosides, sesquiterpenoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pleiotad is typically used in traditional Chinese medicine to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and pills, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
